# ag-shared Sync Log

Migration log for `external/ag-shared/` changes. Each entry documents what changed and what companion actions consuming repos need to perform when syncing.

Newest entries first. Generated by `/ag-shared-sync-log`.

---

## 2026-06-17 -- Shared l10n translation-review script

**Branch:** `at/add-l10n-maintenance-rules`

### Changes

- **[scripts/l10n/review-translations.mjs]** New shared script for the advisory l10n translation-review CI. Dependency-free (raw `fetch` to the Anthropic Messages API, no SDK), product-neutral via the `LOCALE_SRC_DIR` env, and advisory only (never fails the build). Previously duplicated per-repo under `tools/l10n/`; centralised here so a single update percolates to all consuming repos. Consumed by each repo's `.github/workflows/l10n-translation-review.yml`.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Point `.github/workflows/l10n-translation-review.yml` at `external/ag-shared/scripts/l10n/review-translations.mjs` (setting `LOCALE_SRC_DIR` to the repo's locale source dir) and delete the per-repo `tools/l10n/review-translations.mjs` copy.

### Notes

- The script reads `LOCALE_SRC_DIR` (e.g. `packages/ag-charts-locale/src`, `community-modules/locale/src`, `packages/ag-studio-locale/src`), `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `BASE_SHA`, `HEAD_SHA`, `REPORT_PATH`. It no-ops (exit 0) when the API key or base SHA is unset.

---

## 2026-06-12 -- Commit gate: skip symlinks

**Branch:** `ag-16452/charts-switch-to-browser-based-benchmark`
**Ticket:** AG-16452

### Changes

- **[scripts/claude-hooks/precommit-check.sh]** Skip symlinks (staged mode `120000`) in the formatting check. A symlink's staged blob is its target path, not file content, so piping it through prettier always produced a spurious "not formatted" denial â€” e.g. the symlinked `benchmarkUtils.ts` in benchmark example folders.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] None â€” script is consumed in place; takes effect on next sync.

---

## 2026-06-10 -- Batched Claude formatting hooks (track / Stop-format / commit-gate)

**Branch:** `ajt/claude-format-hooks`
**Commits:** `d2ac0d251d..8a86d6b137` (2 commits)

### Changes

- **[scripts/claude-hooks]** New directory of Claude Code hook scripts: `track-edit.sh` (PostToolUse â€” records edited file paths to a per-session list under `$TMPDIR`), `format-tracked.sh` (Stop â€” one batched `nx format` per turn, clears the list on success, surfaces parse errors), `precommit-check.sh` (PreToolUse Bash â€” read-only gate that denies a `git commit` whose staged blobs are not prettier-formatted), plus `_common.sh` helpers and a README.
- **[settings]** `.claude-settings.template.json`: replaced the per-edit `PostToolUse` `nx format --files` hook with the three hooks above (`PostToolUse` â†’ track, `Stop` â†’ format, `PreToolUse` Bash â†’ commit gate).

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh` to regenerate `.claude/settings.json` from the template.
- [ ] Restart Claude Code / your IDE so the new hooks load. `setup-prompts` overwrites `.claude/settings.json`, so any IDE-injected hooks are re-applied on restart.
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh`.

### Notes

Replaces ~2.4â€“3.8s of per-edit `nx format` latency (~4 hrs/month of serialised hook time) with one batched format per turn plus a commit-time check. The commit gate verifies the staged index blob with the repo's own prettier â€” byte-identical to `nx format`, honouring `.prettierignore` â€” and never mutates files. Files `nx format` cannot map to a project (root configs) remain un-auto-formatted, as before.

---

## 2026-06-08 -- Shared JIRA-pipeline glue: install-ag-dev-prompts + pr-preview publish/cleanup

**Branch:** `feat/jira-pipeline-shared-actions`
**Ticket:** AG-17085 (JIRA/GHA agent-pipeline rollout) / ag-grid onboarding

### Changes

- **[github/actions/install-ag-dev-prompts/action.yml]** New composite action. Installs the private `@ag-grid/dev-prompts` npm package from GitHub Packages into `$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/.ag-dev-prompts` so the pipeline *engine* actions shipped inside it (`jira-pipeline`, `jira-resume-preflight`, `jira-ci-success-handler`, `jira-pr-plnkr`, â€¦) can be consumed via local-path `uses:`. Inputs: `channel` (npm dist-tag â€” `latest`/`canary`), `github-token` (default `github.token`), optional `node-version` override. Defaults to the consumer repo's root `package.json` via `node-version-file` so the one action fits every repo. Generalised from ag-charts' repo-local `.github/actions/install-ag-dev-prompts` (which had `node-version: '20'` hardcoded). The engine actions themselves stay on the channel-pinned npm package â€” only this install glue is shared.
- **[github/actions/pr-preview-publish/action.yml]** New composite action. Stages caller-resolved UMD bundle file paths (plus any sibling `<file>.map`) under `pr-<N>/`, writes `_publish/.nojekyll`, and deploys to `gh-pages` via `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3` (`keep_files: true`). Skips cleanly (warning, not error) when no listed bundle exists (tooling-only PRs). Inputs: `pr_number`, `files` (newline-separated), `github_token`, `publish_branch` (default `gh-pages`). The product-specific `build:umd`, artifact handoff, and live-test PR comment stay in each repo's `ci.yml` (served URL differs per repo). Caller owns the `pr-preview-gh-pages` job concurrency group.
- **[github/actions/pr-preview-cleanup/action.yml]** New composite action. Removes a PR's `pr-<N>/` directory from `gh-pages` on PR close (`git rm` + commit + push with rebase-retry). Caller checks out `gh-pages` first with workflow-level `continue-on-error` (the first-run guard â€” `continue-on-error` is unsupported on steps inside composite actions) and gates the action call on it. Inputs: `pr_number`, `publish_branch` (default `gh-pages`).

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Pull ag-shared in each consumer repo (`yarn subrepo pull ag-shared`). The three new actions arrive automatically.
- [ ] **ag-charts (per-repo follow-up):** migrate `.github/workflows/jira-agent-pipeline.yml` to `uses: ./external/ag-shared/github/actions/install-ag-dev-prompts`; replace the `ci.yml` `pr_preview` stage+deploy steps with `pr-preview-publish`; replace the `pr-cleanup.yml` `gh_pages_cleanup` remove step with `pr-preview-cleanup`; delete the now-redundant `.github/actions/install-ag-dev-prompts/`. Behaviour must be unchanged (same `â€¦/ag-charts/pr-<N>/â€¦` URLs).
- [ ] **ag-grid (onboarding, per-repo):** add `.github/workflows/jira-agent-pipeline.yml` (channel `latest`), add a `ci.yml` `pr_preview` job + `pr-cleanup.yml`, all referencing the three shared actions. See ag-dev-prompts `docs/jira-agent-pipeline.md` Â§"Onboarding a new consumer repo" + the onboarding plan.
- [ ] Operator/JIRA wiring per consumer (GitHub App, secrets/vars, GitHub Pages `gh-pages` branch, the `â†’ In Progress â†’ jira-resume` Automation rule).

### Notes

The shared actions do not change any consumer's `ci.yml`/workflows automatically â€” they provide the action surface. The engine actions are intentionally NOT moved here: they ship channel-pinned via the npm package, and `ag-shared` has only a single `latest` branch (no `canary`/`latest` separation). The `jira-agent-pipeline.yml` stub also stays per-repo (GHA cannot `uses:` a workflow from a subrepo path).

---

## 2026-05-18 -- Consolidate Slack CI notification into shared action

**Branch:** `AG-3390-consolidate-staging-notification`
**Ticket:** AG-3390

### Changes

- **[github/actions/slack-ci-notification/action.yml]** New composite GitHub Action that wraps the Slack notification flow. Inputs cover token, Notion lookup credentials, target channels, project name, job statuses, last-successful SHA, report URL, changed-state flag, and deploy-to-staging flag. Replaces the per-repo `scripts/agBotSlackMessage.ts` invocation.
- **[scripts/slack/_ci-notification-utils.mjs]** Shared utility module: workflow context parsing, Slack message construction, channel resolution, and failure classification.
- **[scripts/slack/notify-job-status.mjs]** Job-status notifier used by the composite action when CI completes.
- **[scripts/slack/notify-staging-deploy.mjs]** Staging-deploy notifier gated on docs deploy success.
- **[scripts/slack/get-slack-user-config.mjs + get-slack-user-config-command.mjs]** Notion-backed lookup that resolves GitHub usernames to Slack IDs with caching and error handling.
- **[.claude-settings.template.json]** Enable `ag-eng@ag-dev` and `ag-product@ag-dev` plugins by default.
- **[package.json]** Bump TypeScript dev-dep `^5.4.5` â†’ `^5.8.3` (paired with the TS 5.8 / Angular 20 upgrade landing across consumer repos).

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Pull ag-shared in each consumer repo (`yarn subrepo pull ag-shared`). The new action and scripts arrive automatically.
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh` to pick up `ag-eng` and `ag-product` plugin enablement in `.claude/settings.json`.
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh` to confirm rulesync integrity.
- [ ] **Per-repo follow-up (not part of this sync):** migrate the consumer's `.github/workflows/ci.yml` Slack step to call `./external/ag-shared/github/actions/slack-ci-notification` (see ag-grid PR for the canonical example).
- [ ] **Per-repo follow-up:** ensure repository secrets `SLACK_USER_CONFIG_NOTION_API_TOKEN` and `SLACK_USER_CONFIG_NOTION_DATA_SOURCE_ID` are configured before switching ci.yml over.

### Notes

The shared action does not replace each repo's `ci.yml` automatically â€” it provides the action surface. The ag-grid PR (https://github.com/ag-grid/ag-grid/pull/13793) demonstrates the full migration; ag-charts and ag-studio will land their `ci.yml` migrations in follow-up PRs once their secrets are configured.

The ag-studio sync hit local subrepo commit conflicts during this sync (snyk-related `.snyk`, `scripts/snyk/snykClean.mjs`, and upstream renames of `prompts/guides/code-quality.md` â†’ skill, `prompts/skills/jira/SKILL.md`). The ag-studio pull needs manual rebase resolution before the next sync attempt.

## 2026-04-23 -- Worktree create fast path

**Branch:** `ajt/worktree-create-fast-path`
**Ticket:** none

### Changes

- **[scripts/setup-worktree/preinstall-worktree.sh]**
    -   `clone_directory()` uses a single `clonefile(2)` syscall on macOS for whole-tree O(1) COW â€” ~8Ă— faster than `cp -cR` for large node_modules / .nx trees. Falls back to `cp -cR` then `rsync` on non-macOS or when the destination already exists.
    -   `REPO_ROOT` prefers `$PWD` when it contains a `package.json`, falling back to the `BASH_SOURCE`-derived path. Fixes mode misdetection when the script is invoked from a worktree via the `external/ag-shared` symlink (previous behaviour resolved back to the main repo and reported `Mode: local`).
    -   New `hash_workspace_layout()` + `workspace_layouts_match()` â€” compares glob-resolved workspace `package.json` sets between source and worktree. Catches workspace additions that leave `yarn.lock` untouched.
    -   New `source_has_all_plugin_dists()` â€” iterates `plugins/*/project.json`, and for each plugin with a `build` target, requires a corresponding source `dist/`. Refuses the fast path if any is missing, so a `yarn nx clean`ed source never produces a silent fast-path skip of plugin outputs.
    -   New `try_cow_clone_plugin_dists()` â€” COW-clones the gitignored `plugins/*/dist` outputs (redundant on macOS where the top-level clone already recurses; belt-and-braces for the `rsync` fallback).
    -   Writes `node_modules/.ag-worktree-fast-path-ok` marker only when lockfile parity, workspace topology, plugin-dist completeness, and every COW clone succeed. `clear_fast_path_marker` wipes it on every run so stale markers cannot persist.
- **[scripts/setup-worktree/claude-worktree-create.sh]**
    -   Invokes `preinstall-worktree.sh` directly against the worktree instead of relying on `yarn install` as the driver.
    -   Fast path (marker present): skips `yarn install` and runs `AG_WORKTREE_FAST_PATH=1 yarn run postinstall`. Expensive postinstall steps self-skip via the env var; cheap idempotent steps (`setup-git-hooks`, `setup-prompts`) still run. Future postinstall steps run by default unless the consumer repo explicitly gates them.
    -   Slow path (marker absent): runs `yarn install --offline --frozen-lockfile` with `AG_PREINSTALL_ACTIVE=1` so the preinstall hook does not duplicate the COW work. Falls back to `--prefer-offline` if the offline path fails.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Next `yarn subrepo pull ag-shared` picks up the script changes automatically; no per-repo script action.
- [ ] **Optional â€” to realise the full speedup in consuming repos**, gate expensive postinstall steps on `AG_WORKTREE_FAST_PATH=1` in the consumer's `package.json`. Example:
    ```json
    "postinstall:plugin-build": "[ \"$AG_WORKTREE_FAST_PATH\" = \"1\" ] || nx run-many -p tag:plugin -t build"
    ```
    Candidates are any postinstall step that recreates state the COW clone has already produced: plugin builds, Nx workspace-data wipes/daemon restarts, patch re-application. Leave idempotent/cheap steps (git hooks, prompts staging) ungated. Not applying the gates is safe â€” those steps simply run redundantly on the fast path, narrowing the saving but producing a correct state.
- [ ] On macOS, verify that `python3` is available on `PATH` â€” the new `clone_directory()` fast path shells out to it once for the `clonefile(2)` syscall. Falls back to `cp -cR` if absent, so no action required unless you want the fastest path.

### Notes

On ag-charts the hook time drops from ~100s to ~14â€“20s for a clean worktree from `latest` (node_modules parity, no source edits). Breakdown: `clonefile(2)` trims the `cp -cR` cost from ~36s to ~4s for `node_modules`; skipping `postinstall:plugin-build` trims another ~22s (the cost comes from `postinstall:nx-daemon-reset-metadata` wiping `.nx/workspace-data/` and forcing a cold daemon startup, not from the plugin `tsc` runs themselves); skipping `yarn install`'s validation trims ~8â€“10s.

See `ag-grid/ag-charts#6654` for the original change and measurements.

---

## 2026-04-21 -- Retire sync-to-plugin

**Branch:** `AG-17098/retire-sync-to-plugin`
**Ticket:** AG-17098

### Changes

- **[scripts/sync-to-plugin]** Removed `sync-to-plugin.sh` and `reorganize-to-plugins.py`. The filter-repo-based monolithic reorganisation flow is no longer the path content takes from ag-shared to ag-dev-prompts â€” post-AG-17085 Phase 4, edits are direct commits against `ag-grid/ag-dev-prompts` and `stage.py` fetches from there.
- **[.claude-plugin]** Removed `external/ag-shared/.claude-plugin/plugin-assignments.json` (and the now-empty `.claude-plugin/` directory). The authoritative manifest lives in `ag-grid/ag-dev-prompts/.claude-plugin/plugin-assignments.json`; `stage.py` + `cleanup-plugin-delivered.py` both read from the cache copy at `~/.cache/ag-dev-prompts/repo/.claude-plugin/plugin-assignments.json`, not the subrepo copy.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Next `yarn subrepo pull ag-shared`; no other per-repo action. Consumer repos gain ~500 lines of deletions and lose two scripts that were never invoked from the current pipeline.

### Notes

Closes the "Retire `external/ag-shared/scripts/sync-to-plugin/` â€” no longer primary delivery path; safety net only" item tracked in the `/migrate-to-ag-plugin` skill. Content editing in ag-dev-prompts has been stable for a full cycle (PRs A/B/D/E landed via direct commits) and the old filter-repo path has not been exercised since Phase 4.

## 2026-04-21 -- stage.py: stale cleanup preserves tracked local files

**Branch:** `AG-17098/stage-preserve-tracked`
**Ticket:** AG-17098

### Changes

- **[scripts/rulesync-fetch]** `stage.py` stale-cleanup pass now skips paths that git tracks. Previous behaviour would delete any file whose name appears in the full unfiltered manifest but not in the filtered staged set â€” which includes genuine local rules that happen to share a filename with a plugin-contributed guide. New `_tracked_files()` helper runs `git ls-files .rulesync/` and the cleanup loop logs `keeping tracked <path> (not a stage-leftover)` for matches, preserving the file on disk.
- Handles non-git environments gracefully: if `git` fails (e.g. sandbox without a repo), returns an empty set â€” falls back to prior behaviour for ad-hoc runs.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Next `yarn subrepo pull ag-shared` picks this up automatically; no per-repo action.
- [ ] If any developer's local `.rulesync/rules/*.md` was deleted by the previous stage.py run, restore from git: `git restore .rulesync/rules/<name>.md`.

### Notes

Caught during the ag-grid consumer-repo migration (ag-grid/ag-grid#13576). ag-grid tracks a `.rulesync/rules/benchmarks.md` / `testing.md` / `examples.md` / `docs-pages.md` â€” ag-grid-specific content. The ag-charts plugin ships guides with the same filenames. After the product filter landed (ag-grid/ag-shared#5), the ag-charts files stopped staging, so stale-cleanup saw them as removable â€” and deleted the tracked ag-grid versions. This fix scopes stale-cleanup to files not under git control, keeping local content authoritative.

---

## 2026-04-21 -- stage.py: product-scope plugin staging

**Branch:** `AG-17098/product-filter-stage`
**Ticket:** AG-17098

### Changes

- **[scripts/rulesync-fetch]** `stage.py` now filters the plugin-assignments manifest by product before staging into `.rulesync/`. `PRODUCT_PLUGINS = {ag-charts, ag-grid, ag-studio}`; generic plugins (`ag-core`, `ag-prodeng`) always stage; a product plugin stages iff its name matches the current repo's product. Product detection mirrors `setup-prompts.sh copy_extra_configs`: `$AG_PRODUCT` env var first, falling back to the workspace root's `package.json` `.name` field. Fails loudly if the name is not one of the known products.
- **[scripts/rulesync-fetch]** `stage()` now performs a stale-cleanup pass: after staging, it computes the full-manifest staging set and removes from `.rulesync/{skills,subagents,commands,rules}/` anything in the full set that isn't in the filtered set. Catches other-product content left over from previous runs (pre-filter or after a product switch).
- **[scripts/rulesync-fetch]** `list_staged()` gained `filter_by_product` kwarg (default True); the `--list` CLI honours the filter so `setup-prompts.sh` surfaces only the consuming product's staged paths. Internal helper `_stageable_paths(plugins)` factored out and reused by both the stale-cleanup diff and `list_staged()`.

### Migration Actions

Per consuming repo on next `yarn subrepo pull ag-shared`:

- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`. The stale-cleanup pass will drop any other-product content previously staged into `.rulesync/skills/`, `.rulesync/subagents/`, etc. (e.g. in the ag-grid checkout, ag-charts plugin items land for removal). Expect a one-time chunky diff in the follow-up commit.
- [ ] Restart any running Claude Code session â€” no behaviour change on the Claude side (it reads plugins directly), but Cursor / Codex / Gemini / AGENTS.md will stop seeing other-product items.

### Notes

Closes the follow-up flagged on ag-grid/ag-grid#13576. Before this change, `stage.py` copied every plugin's content from the manifest regardless of the consuming product â€” for Claude this was harmless (targets exclude `claudecode`), but non-Claude tools in an ag-grid checkout surfaced ag-charts-specific skills/agents/commands as first-class options, and vice versa.

The filter keys on the same workspace `package.json` `.name` field already used by `scripts/sonar/sync-sonar-issues.ts` and `setup-prompts.sh`, so there's one authoritative product source across the tooling.

---

## 2026-04-21 -- Templatise `.claude-settings.json` for per-product `enabledPlugins`

**Branch:** `AG-17098/templatise-claude-settings`
**Ticket:** AG-17098

### Changes

- **[settings]** `.claude-settings.json` replaced by `.claude-settings.template.json`. The `enabledPlugins` block now contains a `${PRODUCT}@ag-dev` placeholder alongside the always-on `ag-core@ag-dev` and `ag-prodeng@ag-dev` entries. Rendered to `.claude/settings.json` (real file, not symlink) by `setup-prompts.sh`.
- **[scripts/setup-prompts]** `copy_extra_configs` in `setup-prompts.sh` now renders the template via `sed` substitution. Product identifier is read from `$AG_PRODUCT` env var first, falling back to the workspace root's `package.json` `name` field â€” the same detection source `scripts/sonar/sync-sonar-issues.ts` already uses. Fails loudly if the detected name is not one of `ag-charts | ag-grid | ag-studio`. Stale symlinks at the destination are replaced with a real file atomically.
- **[scripts/sync-to-plugin]** `sync-to-plugin.sh` `EXCLUDE_FILES` list updated to exclude the new template filename.
- **[.claude-plugin]** `plugin-assignments.json` `excludedFromPlugin` note updated to mention the new template filename.

### Migration Actions

Per consuming repo (ag-charts first, then ag-grid and ag-studio once their rollouts begin):

- [ ] `yarn subrepo pull ag-shared`. No new marker file needed â€” the workspace root `package.json` `name` is authoritative (already `ag-charts` / `ag-grid` / `ag-studio`).
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`. Confirm `.claude/settings.json` is a real file (not a symlink) and contains the correct `${PRODUCT}@ag-dev: true` entry for this repo's product.
- [ ] Restart any running Claude Code session so the rendered settings take effect.
- [ ] No `.gitignore` change needed â€” the repo root `.claude/` directory is already gitignored in all three consuming repos.

### Notes

This change unblocks AG-17098 (ag-grid + ag-studio rollout). Before this, `.claude-settings.json` hardcoded `ag-charts@ag-dev: true`, forcing divergence if ag-grid or ag-studio pulled the subrepo. The template + renderer keeps the shared file as the single source of truth while letting each consumer pick its product plugin deterministically.

Detection uses `package.json` `name` because it is already the product-identification source elsewhere in ag-shared tooling (`scripts/sonar/sync-sonar-issues.ts` `PRODUCT_SONAR_KEYS`). Using the same convention here avoids adding a redundant marker file and removes any risk of the two sources drifting.

The substitution is a simple `sed "s|\${PRODUCT}|<name>|g"`. Product names are alphanumeric + hyphen only so there are no escaping concerns. The `.claude-settings.template.json` file is not valid JSON as-is (the placeholder is in a key position); editors may flag it â€” that's expected.

---

## 2026-04-21 -- rulesync-fetch: HTTPS by default, gh auto-auth

**Branch:** `ag-17085/fix-ag-dev-prompts-access`
**Ticket:** AG-17085

### Changes

- **[scripts/rulesync-fetch]** `fetch.sh` default auth path switched from SSH to HTTPS. Developers whose SSH key is not registered with GitHub but who are authenticated via `gh` or a credential helper (osxkeychain, manager, libsecret) now clone successfully without extra setup. Auth precedence is now: (1) `AG_DEV_PROMPTS_REPO` override, (2) `GITHUB_TOKEN`, (3) `gh auth token` if `gh` is installed, (4) plain HTTPS via credential helper. SSH is still reachable by setting `AG_DEV_PROMPTS_REPO=git@github.com:ag-grid/ag-dev-prompts.git`.
- **[scripts/rulesync-fetch]** Remediation output now covers the `https` and `gh` paths and points SSH users at the override rather than treating SSH as the happy path.
- **[scripts/setup-prompts]** Stale comment in `setup-prompts.sh` referencing the old auth path names updated.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Re-run `yarn` (or `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`). No config changes required â€” the new default Just Works for most dev setups.

### Notes

Prompted by a reproducible failure on a dev machine with no GitHub-registered SSH key but working `gh` auth. Tokens are never persisted to `.git/config`; they are passed via `-c http.extraHeader=Authorization: Basic â€¦` for the single invocation only.

---

## 2026-04-21 -- Rename ag-shared plugin to ag-prodeng (v1.0.0-beta.1)

**Branch:** `ag-17085/claude-plugin-rework`
**Ticket:** AG-17085

### Changes

- **[settings]** `.claude-settings.json` `enabledPlugins` key renamed from `ag-shared@ag-dev` â†’ `ag-prodeng@ag-dev`. Without this, Claude Code cannot resolve the plugin in the `ag-dev` marketplace and silently drops all product-engineering skills/agents/commands.
- **[.claude-plugin]** `plugin-assignments.json` synced to upstream ag-dev-prompts `1.0.0-beta.1`: plugin key renamed (`ag-shared` â†’ `ag-prodeng`), docs-review pattern inverted (generic methodology moved to `ag-prodeng`; product configs under `ag-charts`), four AG Charts-specific artefacts moved from `ag-prodeng` â†’ `ag-charts` (`sonar-fix` skill; `prepare-release-notes`, `release-blog-writer`, `release-options-review` commands).
- **[github]** `github/actions/codex-pr-review/action.yml` updated to load `pr-review/SKILL.md` from `plugins/ag-prodeng/` instead of `plugins/ag-shared/`.
- **[docs]** `.rulesync/README.md` updated to reference the new plugin name in the channel table and release-summary note.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh` to re-symlink `.claude/settings.json` with the new `enabledPlugins` key.
- [ ] Restart any running Claude Code session so the new marketplace entry is picked up.
- [ ] Anyone who previously `/plugin enable`d `ag-shared@ag-dev` manually should `/plugin disable ag-shared@ag-dev` â€” the new `ag-prodeng@ag-dev` is enabled automatically via `.claude-settings.json`.
- [ ] Slash-command namespaces change: `/ag-shared:git-conventions` â†’ `/ag-prodeng:git-conventions`, `/ag-shared:jira` â†’ `/ag-prodeng:jira`, `/ag-shared:pr-review` â†’ `/ag-prodeng:pr-review`, etc. Muscle-memory invocations under the old namespace will fail.

### Notes

Plugin renamed to reflect its actual remit (product engineering across ag-charts/ag-grid/ag-studio), not simply "things shared from ag-shared". The `ag-shared` git-subrepo directory and associated skills (`ag-shared-sync-log`, `sync-ag-shared`) keep their names â€” they refer to the subrepo, not the plugin.

---

## 2026-04-20 -- Collapse six-plugin layout into ag-core + ag-shared + ag-charts (v0.5.0-beta.1)

**Branch:** `ag-17085/claude-plugin-rework`
**Ticket:** AG-17085

### Changes

- **[settings]** `.claude-settings.json` `enabledPlugins` reduced from six entries (`ag-core`, `ag-prompt`, `ag-pm`, `ag-eng`, `ag-rel`, `ag-charts`) to three (`ag-core`, `ag-shared`, `ag-charts`).
- **[scripts/sync-to-plugin]** `plugin-assignments.json` rewritten: merges `ag-prompt` + `ag-pm` + `ag-eng` + `ag-rel` into a single `ag-shared` plugin. `plan-review` and `plan-verify` skills move from `ag-eng` â†’ `ag-core` (product-agnostic). Version bumped to `0.5.0-beta.1` â€” beta tag stays until every downstream repo has migrated.
- **[scripts/setup-prompts]** Fixed `cleanup-plugin-delivered.py` default manifest path (was pointing at a stale `external/ag-shared/prompts/.claude-plugin/â€¦` path that doesn't exist, which silently disabled cleanup). Same fix applied to the `setup-prompts.sh` conditional that gates the cleanup call.
- **[github]** `github/actions/codex-pr-review/action.yml` updated to load the `pr-review` skill from `plugins/ag-shared/` instead of `plugins/ag-eng/`.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh`
- [ ] After sync to `ag-dev-prompts`, manually `git rm` the now-stale `plugins/ag-prompt/`, `plugins/ag-pm/`, `plugins/ag-eng/`, `plugins/ag-rel/` directories on that repo â€” the reorganise step only creates plugins in the current manifest, it does not remove absent ones.
- [ ] In downstream repos (ag-grid, ag-studio), anyone who manually `/plugin enable`d the retired plugins should `/plugin disable ag-eng@ag-dev` etc. â€” enabling the new `ag-shared@ag-dev` is handled automatically by `.claude-settings.json`.
- [ ] Review the user-level `/migrate-to-ag-plugin` skill (outside this repo) â€” confirm it iterates the manifest rather than hardcoding the retired plugin names.

### Notes

Namespaces change: items previously invoked as `/ag-eng:git-conventions`, `/ag-pm:jira`, `/ag-rel:prepare-release-notes`, `/ag-prompt:rulesync` now live under `/ag-shared:*`. `/ag-core:plan-review` and `/ag-core:plan-verify` are the new homes for the plan review/verify skills. `ag-charts` contents are unchanged.

Beta tag (`-beta.N`) is intentional â€” drop it (â†’ `0.5.0`) only after all three consuming repos (ag-charts, ag-grid, ag-studio) have landed the migration and the layout is confirmed stable. Subsequent iterations during the beta window bump the beta counter.

---

## 2026-04-01 -- Fix worktree .yarnrc leak, add CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER, enhance /pr-create --monitor

**Branch:** `ajt/agentic-tooling-fixes`
**PR:** https://github.com/ag-grid/ag-charts/pull/6562

### Changes

- **[scripts/setup-worktree]** Fixed `claude-worktree-create.sh` setting `AG_CLOUD_INSTALL=1`, which caused `preinstall-worktree.sh` to run in "cloud" mode and create a superfluous `.yarnrc` in local worktrees. Local worktrees now correctly use "worktree" mode (COW clone + symlink fix only).
- **[prompts/config]** Added `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` and `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` to the `env` section of `.claude-settings.json`.
- **[prompts/skills]** Enhanced `/pr-create` with `--monitor` option (STEP 9): polls CI status after PR creation, automatically fixes straightforward failures (lint, formatting, merge conflicts), and reviews PR feedback proposing fixes for the user to explicitly accept or reject.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh`
- [ ] Delete any stale `.yarnrc` files from existing worktrees under `~/.worktrees/`

### Notes

No new symlinks or skills to register. The `.claude-settings.json` changes are picked up automatically by `setup-prompts.sh`. The `/pr-create --monitor` enhancement is backwards-compatible â€” without `--monitor`, behaviour is unchanged.

---

## 2026-03-30 -- Add /design-review skill and AGENTS.md tooling health check

**Branch:** `ajt/review-skill`
**PR:** https://github.com/ag-grid/ag-charts/pull/6546

### Changes

- **[prompts/skills]** Added `design-review` skill â€” multi-agent expert panel for reviewing design documents. Dynamically derives domain-specific reviewer roles from the document's content; always includes Tech Lead, Product Manager, and Devil's Advocate. Supports `--quick` (4 agents) and `--thorough` (6-8 agents) modes. User reviews all feedback before incorporation.
- **[AGENTS.md]** Added "Tooling Health Check" section â€” on first response only, verifies canary skills are present and displays a one-time warning if agentic tooling is not initialised.
- **[prompts/guides]** Added `rulesync-editing.md` rule â€” activates on edits to `.rulesync/`, `.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.github/copilot*`, `.codex/`, `.gemini/`, `.opencode/`, `AGENTS.md`, or `CLAUDE.md`. Steers the agent to treat `.rulesync/` as source of truth and load `/rulesync` for the full reference.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] ag-grid: Add `.rulesync/skills/design-review` directory symlink â†’ `../../external/ag-shared/prompts/skills/design-review/`
- [ ] ag-studio: Add `.rulesync/skills/design-review` directory symlink â†’ `../../external/ag-shared/prompts/skills/design-review/`
- [ ] ag-grid: Add `.rulesync/rules/rulesync-editing.md` symlink â†’ `../../external/ag-shared/prompts/guides/rulesync-editing.md`
- [ ] ag-studio: Add `.rulesync/rules/rulesync-editing.md` symlink â†’ `../../external/ag-shared/prompts/guides/rulesync-editing.md`
- [ ] ag-grid: Add "Tooling Health Check" section to `AGENTS.md` (see below)
- [ ] ag-studio: Add "Tooling Health Check" section to `AGENTS.md` (see below)
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh`

### AGENTS.md section to add

Insert after the "Pre-Commit Requirements" section (or equivalent build/test commands section):

```markdown
## Tooling Health Check

On the **first response** of a conversation, verify that project skills are available by checking the system-reminder skill list. If **any** of the canary skills are missing, display a one-time warning before doing anything else. Do not repeat the warning on subsequent responses.

**Canary skills:** `example`, `dev-server`, `debug`, `git-conventions`, `jira`, `simplify`

**Warning to display (if any canary skill is missing):**

> **Agentic tooling is not initialised.** Expected skills (example, dev-server, debug, git-conventions, jira, simplify) are missing or incomplete. Run `yarn` from the repository root to set up AI tooling configuration, then restart your session. If you are in a worktree, ensure you ran `yarn` in the worktree directory (not just the main checkout).

Continue assisting the user after displaying the warning.
```

## 2026-03-30 -- Add "NEVER guess" ambiguity rule to agent instructions

**Branch:** `ajt/add-ambiguity-rule-to-agent-instructions`
**PR:** https://github.com/ag-grid/ag-charts/pull/6540

### Changes

- **[AGENTS.md, .rulesync/rules/ag-charts.md]** Added "Ambiguity and confidence" section instructing AI agents to never guess when requirements or details are ambiguous â€” ask for guidance or do more research instead.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] ag-grid: Add the same "Ambiguity and confidence" section to `AGENTS.md` and the project-specific root rule (`.rulesync/rules/ag-grid.md` or equivalent), immediately after the `# AI Agent Instructions` heading
- [ ] ag-studio: Add the same "Ambiguity and confidence" section to `AGENTS.md` and the project-specific root rule, immediately after the `# AI Agent Instructions` heading

### Section to add

```markdown
## Ambiguity and confidence

**NEVER guess:** This is the number one rule; if requirements, facts or details are ambiguous it is much better to admit this and ask for guidance (with context on why the ambiguity where possible) or do more research to ground things out, than to guess with a risk of being wrong.
```

## 2026-03-25 -- Add JIRA Agile ranking API guidance and code-quality design principles

**Branch:** `bls/triage-rt-board-skill`
**Commits:** `590a6207f0..HEAD` (4 commits)

### Changes

- **[prompts/skills]** `jira` â€” Added "Ranking Issues (Agile API)" section documenting the `PUT /rest/agile/1.0/issue/rank` endpoint, including rank custom field ID (`10120`), auth, batch semantics, and verification steps. The standard MCP `editJiraIssue` tool silently ignores rank changes.
- **[prompts/guides]** `code-quality` â€” Added "Design and Modularity" section covering single responsibility, small focused functions, composition over inheritance, dependency direction, file organisation, minimal public surface, and pure function preference.

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh`

### Notes

The new code-quality "Design and Modularity" principles are paired with a pre-commit self-review checklist added to the ag-charts root rule (`.rulesync/rules/ag-charts.md` â†’ `CLAUDE.md`). Other consuming repos should consider adding a similar checklist to their project-specific root rule (`.rulesync/rules/<project-name>.md`) so the quality gate appears in their `CLAUDE.md` too.

## 2026-03-09 -- Rulesync skill, worktree hooks, skill optimisations

**Branch:** `latest`
**Commits:** `c2255312a5..1d93d98522` (8 commits)

### Changes

- **[prompts/skills]** Added `rulesync` skill to replace the `rulesync` guide â€” comprehensive configuration reference for `.rulesync/` directory management
- **[prompts/guides]** Removed `rulesync.md` guide (superseded by the `rulesync` skill)
- **[prompts/skills]** Optimised `dev-server` skill â€” added multi-repo URL table, troubleshooting section, removed redundant `_dev-server-core.md` partial
- **[prompts/skills]** Optimised `plunker` skill â€” extracted CSS into `assets/ag-example-styles.css`, slimmed guide, improved description
- **[prompts/skills]** Enhanced `git-conventions` skill â€” expanded description for better auto-triggering, added PR base branch guidance
- **[prompts/skills]** Enhanced `pr-create` skill â€” improved PR creation guidelines
- **[prompts/config]** Added `WorktreeCreate` and `WorktreeRemove` hooks to `.claude-settings.json` for automated worktree lifecycle management
- **[scripts/setup-prompts]** Updated `verify-rulesync.sh` to include files in skill subdirectories (e.g. `assets/`) in the expected inventory
- **[scripts]** Added `scripts/setup-worktree/claude-worktree-create.sh` and `claude-worktree-remove.sh` for Claude Code worktree lifecycle

### Migration Actions

- [ ] Remove `.rulesync/rules/rulesync.md` file symlink (target guide was deleted)
- [ ] Add `.rulesync/skills/rulesync` directory symlink -> `../../external/ag-shared/prompts/skills/rulesync/`
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/setup-prompts.sh`
- [ ] Run `./external/ag-shared/scripts/setup-prompts/verify-rulesync.sh`

### Post-Migration Verification

- [ ] Confirm `/rulesync` skill is available (type `/rulesync help` in Claude Code)
- [ ] Confirm the old rulesync rule no longer loads (check `.claude/rules/` for stale `rulesync.md`)
- [ ] Test worktree creation: run `/worktree <branch-name>` in Claude Code and verify the hook creates the worktree under `~/.worktrees/<repo>/`
- [ ] Test worktree removal: exit the worktree session and verify cleanup runs without errors
- [ ] Verify `dev-server` skill still triggers correctly (`/dev-server help`)

### Notes

The `rulesync` guide-to-skill migration is a structural change: consuming repos that had a `.rulesync/rules/rulesync.md` file symlink pointing to `../../external/ag-shared/prompts/guides/rulesync.md` must remove it (the target no longer exists) and add a directory symlink at `.rulesync/skills/rulesync` instead. The new worktree hook scripts (`scripts/setup-worktree/`) are referenced from `.claude-settings.json` and do not need `.rulesync/` symlinks.
